_description: Airline crew roster activity/duty code legend. Codes are AIRLINE/SYSTEM-specific (no industry standard except SSIM for schedules). 'category' is normalized; 'is_off' marks non-working days for RaiLink's sharing layer. Extracted from sample rosters' own legend boxes + research. Verify per operator.

air_premia_and_jeju_grid.vendor: grid/calendar format (Air Premia printed grid; Jeju Air via CrewConnex app)
seen_in: sample 1 = Air Premia (YP) printed month grid
sample 2 = Jeju Air CrewConnex app (4V codes, Oct20 colored grid)
air_premia_and_jeju_grid.format: calendar grid; cell = code + 1-2 flight numbers + ONE clock-time range. NO sector shown. Full month on one page (Air Premia) or scrolling app view (Jeju CrewConnex).

OVMRIG.note: Research suggests OVMRIG commonly = over-max-rig pay flag elsewhere; this operator uses it for marriage leave. Confirms codes are per-airline.

arms.format: per-day scrolling list. Day header shows BRF (briefing). Each flight card: flight_no + sector(ICN-TPE) + times. NO aircraft type. Detail behind '>' (hidden).

LEAVE.label_en: Leave; printed as 'LEAVE - <suffix>' (HV0 / ALB / PVC), full day 00:00-23:59
LEAVE.note: Suffix meaning unconfirmed (likely leave-type or station code). Treat whole day as off.

DH.label_en: Deadhead / positioning as pax; printed 'DH DH-FLT (LJxxx)'

BRF.label_en: Briefing/report time (duty header attribute, not a duty)

_car_icon.label_en: Ground transport provided between airports (e.g. GMP<->ICN)

asiana_table.format: table: DATE | FLIGHT | SHOWUP | SECTOR | STD | STA | ETD. Richest single view: has report time + sector + multi-leg. Layover shown as station-only rows.

_station_only_row.label_en: A row with only a station code (FRA/BKK/ICN) and no flight = layover/position marker; bridges outbound and return legs
_station_only_row.note: Key derivation signal: crew is at that station overnight.

_showup_format.label_en: SHOWUP like 'T2 04 10:40' = Terminal 2, day 04, report 10:40; 'G 07 11:25' = gate/other, day 07, 11:25

research_standard_codes._note: Cross-airline concepts (regulator-defined), mnemonics vary. Use to seed normalization.
research_standard_codes.OVMRIG: Over-max-rig pay flag (but per-airline; sample1 = marriage leave)
